text:
There lived an old turtle named Olga. She spent her days sunning herself on the sandy shore, occasionally listening to the ocean breezes.

One day, a whirlpool formed beneath her. Olga did not fear it; after all, she'd danced on waves before. She waddled along.

Suddenly, something big snapped her shell! She rolled onto her back.

Across the water popped a majestic elephant.

"What has got into Olga?"

"I've never seen such a sturdy turtle," puffed the elephant.

"I'll bet you haven't," chuckled Olga. "But I could give you a few moves."

And they danced. Olga moonlit waltzed across the waves. The elephant learned the turtle shimmy! They laughed together, until the elephant had danced sufficiently.

After they parted ways, the elephant paused.

"Do you think I could learn THAT trick?"

"Oh, definitely," Olga assured.

The elephant returned each day, each time mastering a step, until he
